observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to – a) reconstitute the Inventory Committee to address the need to conduct a physical count of all inventory to establish the correct balances of the inventory accounts and to be able to prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RCPI); b) direct the General Service Office:
• in coordination with MHO and MSWD to prepare the Inspection and Acceptance Report of all the donated supplies/inventories received by the Municipality and submit it together with a copy of the Property Transfer Receipt (PTR) to the Accounting Office for recording; and
• to regularly submit to the Accounting Office relevant reports regarding the distributions/issuances of supplies/inventories to end-users; and
c) direct the Municipal Accountant to:
• abide by the provisions of the MNGAS on the use of the Moving Average Method of costing the inventory;
• record all the donated supplies/inventories;
• observe the accrual method of accounting to timely record the distributions/ issuances of supplies/inventories to end-users; and
• make an adjusting entry, based on valid supporting documents, to exclude the distributed relief goods from the balance of Inventory Accounts to reflect the accurate balance of the inventory.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant used the Moving Average Method of costing the inventory.

The Municipal Accountant prepared adjusting entry. See Observation (JEV No. 100-24-01-016 dated January 31, 2024)

We recommended that Management:
Enjoin the Municipal Accountant to promptly reconcile bank versus book balance in order to identify the reconciling items and prepare necessary correcting entries.
Submit to the Audit Team the revised and duly reconciled Bank Reconciliation Statement, together with all pertinent supporting documents and the corresponding journal entry vouchers for any adjustments made to the account for review.
Enjoin the Municipal Accountant to immediately review the book balances and justify the omission and/or inclusion of the amounts in question and submit the relevant documents to the Audit Team. If warranted, prepare adjusting entries for all the deficiencies/ inaccuracies discussed above.

We recommended that the Management:
Allot/ reserve a storage room or area to keep all purchased and available supplies and inventory.
Ensure that the semestral count of Inventory items is promptly conducted, and the corresponding report is submitted on time to the Audit Team.
Strictly implement proper Inventory Management as prescribed in the foregoing Manual.
Enjoin the Municipal Accountant, Inventory Committee and Supply Office to reconcile their books and records.
Enjoin the Municipal Accountant to maintain Ledger Cards for proper monitoring of Inventory.

We recommended that Management:
Enjoin the Inventory Committee to identify the cause of the variance between the physically counted properties and the accounting records to make the necessary reconciliation and adjustments;
Enjoin the Inventory Committee to include in their physical count, not only the Land and movable properties but also other immovable properties of the LGU and submit the complete RPCPPE to the Audit Team; and,
Create a committee composed of one personnel each from the Treasurer’s Office, Municipal Engineer’s Office and Accounting Office who shall reconcile their respective records of PPE and at the same time maintain updated and complete PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards.

We recommended that Management enjoin the Municipal Accountant to:
Promptly correct the journal entries and revert the deduction in the TL-MDRRM account; and,
Identify the items/ transactions pertaining to the LGU’s DRRM activities that were charged to the General Fund instead of the TF-DRRM to serve as basis for the adjustment and submit the same to the Audit Team for audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, in his capacity as the Chairman of the Local School Board of Lupi:
Direct the LSB to furnish the Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant, and Municipal Treasurer a copy of the SEF Budget not later than seven (7) days after its approval as the basis for disbursement or certification of availability of funds and for recording purposes;
Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the quarterly and annual utilization reports and post them to the LGU’s website, if there is any, and to three conspicuous places as required in the Joint Circular; and,
Instruct the LSB to submit the SEF utilization reports to the DepEd Central Office, through appropriate channels, not later than the 20th day after the end of the quarter for the 1st to 3rd quarter reports, and not later than the 15th day of February of the following year for the annual report, copy furnished the Sangguniang Bayan and respective Regional Offices of the DBM and DILG.

We recommended that the Management:
Submit written justification on:
why the foreign travel on international benchmarking was allowed when it did not fall under any of the categories of foreign travels authorized under the EO, and
on the role(s) or participation of each member in attending the aforementioned international benchmarking based on their responsibilities and obligation in the Agency.
Submit all the necessary liquidation reports, along with the required documents.
Ensure compliance with EO No. 77 and other relevant rules and regulations on the authorized local and foreign travels and observe economy and austerity measures in the use of government funds; and
Ensure compliance with Circular 2012-001 to ensure that all government expenditures are properly supported and justified.

We recommended that the Management:
Revisit the Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 and strictly adhere with its provision to ensure transparency and accountability in government procurement.
Submit to the Office of the Auditors the Sales Invoices for Check Nos. 0001120599 and 0001080453.
Enjoin the Municipal Accounting Office and Bids and Awards Committee to ensure completeness and correctness of documentary requirements as prescribed in COA Circular 2012-001 before disbursement/ payment to suppliers and contractors.

We recommended that the Management:
Require the Local Engineer and/or concerned authorized agency personnel mandate to supervise the implementation of the infrastructure projects to submit to the audit team copies of the Results of Test Analyses/Status of Quality Control Test required by the aforementioned rules and regulations in order to ensure that the completed projects conforms with agency standards, plans and specifications as part of its quality control program.
Require the Local Engineer/BAC to include Minimum Materials Testing Equipment in the Technical Component of the Bid together with the other major equipment as contained in the Technical Envelope which shall be subjected to preliminary examination and further verified during the post-qualification stage.
Henceforth, require the Local Accountant not to pass during pre-audit of the transaction particularly on final payment, disbursement vouchers on infrastructure projects not supported with Results of Test Analyses from DPWH/BRS/DOST/DTI accredited testing laboratories for construction materials and concrete which requires testing to avoid suspensions and/or disallowances in audit.

We recommended that Management:
Prioritize the implementation and completion, preferably within the budget year, of all priority development projects/programs funded out of the 20 per cent EDF to attain the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the Municipality.
Allocate funds for the completion of the cited projects to prevent possible higher repair and construction cost, and to permit immediate utilization of these infrastructure projects.
Avoid partial appropriation of funds for infrastructure projects in cases where partial implementation may compromise the structural integrity of the intended infrastructure, hinder the optimal utilization of public funds, or result in increased repair and additional construction costs in the future.
Enjoin the Municipal Development Council (MDC) and the Municipal Engineer to work together in the formulation of the Agency’s development plan especially those involving infrastructure projects.

We recommended that the Management:
Direct the Municipal Engineer to coordinate with the Contractor and come up with a viable solution to repair the defects as soon as possible, otherwise, impose sanctions and penalties, if warranted.
Require Test Analysis in all infrastructure projects, including the construction of local road networks, to ensure quality of all constructed projects.
Comply with the provisions stated in Section 62 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that the Management:
Revisit GAD related laws and regulations such as PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memo Circular 2013-01 as amended by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memo Circular 2016-01 and strictly adhere to the required policies in preparing/formulating the Annual GPB.
Enjoin implementing offices to prioritize the development of detailed project schedules to support the effective execution of the PPAs. By doing so, the likelihood and feasibility of completing the PPAs within the budgeted year will be significantly increased.
Enjoin the GFPS to formulate Gender Agenda and conduct Gender Analysis. Henceforth, update Agency’s Gender Database.
Properly coordinate with all the personnel/offices involved, and ensure proper monitoring of the GAD PPAs, to be able to implement all GAD PPAs as planned/budgeted to achieve GAD’s objectives and address gender issues.

We recommended that Management enjoin the LCPC Focal Person to strictly adhere to LCPC policies and fully implement LCPC-related activities and institute proper planning of the LCPC PPAs, to be able to realize its full implementation and utilization of the budget as planned/budgeted to achieve LCPC’s objectives.

end to the Municipal Accountant; and (ii) properly accomplish the required
details in the Check Register;
b) Municipal Accountant to prepare journal entry to revert the cash balance of
unreleased checks at year-end and the reversing entry in the ensuing year; and
c) proper coordination between Treasury and Accounting Offices to monitor the
actual status of issued checks so that appropriate adjustments could be made
promptly.

a) Municipal Accounting Office to (i) prepare subsidiary ledgers for RPT and
SET Receivable accounts as required by the MNGAS for LGUs; (ii) coordinate
with the Municipal Treasurer’s Office (MTO) for the reconciliation of their
records; and (iii) prepare adjusting entries, as warranted; and
b) MTO to (i) submit to the Audit Team the updated status of notices sent to the
delinquent taxpayers; (ii) post the notice of delinquency at the main entrance
of the municipal hall and in a publicly accessible and conspicuous place in each
barangay concerned; (iii) publish the notice of delinquency once a week for
two consecutive weeks, in a newspaper of general circulation; (iv) include the
reasons for non-collection/non-payment in the “remarks” portion of the
certified list of tax delinquencies; (v) submit the same to the Sanggunian with

a request for assistance in the enforcement of the remedies for collection; (vi)
coordinate with the Provincial Government of Bataan regarding the technical
assistance on the use of PATAS to utilize the system in the preparation of
RPTAR and certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due
and collectible for the year; and (vii) furnish the Municipal Accounting Office
of its copy which should be the basis in the set-up of receivables at the
beginning of each year.

We recommended and Management agreed to -
a) reconstitute the Inventory Committee to address the need to conduct a
physical count of all inventory to establish the correct balances of the
inventory accounts and to be able to prepare and submit the Report on the
Physical Count of Inventories (RCPI);
b) direct the General Service Office:
• in coordination with MHO and MSWD to prepare the Inspection and
Acceptance Report of all the donated supplies/inventories received by the
Municipality and submit it together with a copy of the Property Transfer
Receipt (PTR) to the Accounting Office for recording; and
• to regularly submit to the Accounting Office relevant reports regarding the
distributions/ issuances of supplies/inventories to end-users; and
c) direct the Municipal Accountant to:
• abide by the provisions of the MNGAS on the use of the Moving Average
Method of costing the inventory;
• record all the donated supplies/inventories;
• observe the accrual method of accounting to timely record the distributions/
issuances of supplies/inventories to end-users; and
• make an adjusting entry, based on valid supporting documents, to exclude
the distributed relief goods from the balance of Inventory Accounts to
reflect the accurate balance of the inventory.

We recommended and Management agreed to reconstitute the Inventory
Committee to address the need to conduct a physical count of all the Municipality’s
PPEs to be able to prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of PPEs
(RCPPE) and to conduct the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts in accordance with
COA Circular No. 2020-006.

agreed to –
a. direct the Municipal Accountant to (i) make necessary entries to record all the
unrecorded PPEs received through donations; and (ii) complete the update of

c. instruct the Appraisal and Disposal Committee to facilitate the disposal of
unserviceable assets pursuant to Section 79 of PD No. 1445.

Accountant to –

We recommended that Management:

Require the concerned officer to maintain and submit the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) together with the corresponding Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) for the issued supplies/goods, and maintain stock cards for each item of Inventory to ensure proper accounting and promote better control in the receipt and issuance of supplies; and
Ensure that the semestral count of Inventory items is promptly conducted, and the corresponding report is submitted on time to the Audit Team.

We recommended that Management:
Create an Inventory Committee that will conduct the complete physical count of the LGU’s properties, plant, and equipment; and,
Require the concerned officers to submit/ present Property records including PPE Ledger Cards, Property Cards, Report on Local Road Network Property Cards for all roads and its components, and the Local Road Network Ledger Card to the audit team.

We recommended that Management enjoin the Municipal Accountant to request for write-off of the receivables which are dormant or remained outstanding for ten years or more following the requirements stated in COA Circular No. 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023.

We recommended that Management enjoin the LDRRM Council to ensure the optimum utilization of its LDRRM Plan and appropriate sufficient funds for every pre-disaster activity so as not to deprive their constituents of the benefits that would be derived from these pre-disaster activities. And, if necessary, reprogram redundant activities to other pre-disaster PPAs.

We recommended that Management prioritize the implementation and completion, preferably within the budget year, of all priority development projects/programs funded out of the 20 per cent EDF to attain the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes of the Municipality.

We recommended that Management enjoin the GAD Focal Person to strictly adhere to GAD policies to implement GAD-related activities and institute proper planning of the GAD PPAs, to be able to realize its full implementation and utilization of the budget as planned/budgeted to achieve GAD’s objectives and address gender issues.

We recommended that Management require the BAC and the concerned officers to:
Attach a checklist of the required documentation to every disbursement voucher to ensure that all required documents are attached thereto; and
Strictly comply with the provisions enumerated in the COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that Management:
Adhere strictly to the rules and regulations of GSIS, PhilHealth, and Pag-IBIG regarding proper withholding of premium payments and remittances thereof;
Verify immediately the cause of the difference between the year-end balance of Due to PhilHealth and the actual billing from the Agency and if warranted, request that the interests and penalties charged to the LGU be waived; and
Enjoin employees to comply with the requirements of GSIS, PhilHealth, and Pag-IBIG to initiate immediate remittance of their premiums and come up with a management plan regarding the unremitted employee’s share whose contracts were not renewed.

Recommendation:
Direct the Municipal Assessor to expedite the revision and submission of the Assessment Roll to the Municipal Treasurer on or before December 31 of each year.
Require the Municipal Treasurer to update the Real Property Tax Account Register/Taxpayer’s index card based on the Assessment Roll and furnish the accountant a certified list of taxes due and collectible at the beginning of each year as a basis for establishing the correct RPT/SET Receivables and the related Deferred RPT/Deferred SET Income Accounts; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to record RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of each year based on the certified list of taxpayers showing the amount of tax due and collectible for the year.

observations.recommendation:
We commend the management for its effort to enforce immediate settlement of
the audit suspensions totaling to ₱ 925,118.48 in accordance with Section 9.1 of
COA Circular No. 94-001 dated January 20, 1994. Henceforth, ensure that
government auditing rules and regulations are strictly complied with before
processing claims/effecting payments to minimize suspensions and disallowances
in audit.

Informing by the Municipal Accountant to all persons responsible and/or liable about
the suspensions on their claim and require them to submit the necessary documents
and/or justification as required in the Notice of Suspensions with a period of ninety
(90) days from receipt of the notices; otherwise the suspension shall mature into
disallowance.

Continue the efforts of the Municipality in implementing the Gender and
Development Plan and Budget for CY 2019 in accordance with Section 4.4 of DBM
- NEDA -NCRFW Joint Circular No. 2004 -1 dated April 5, 2004.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant should prepare, update and
submit the Bank Reconciliation Statement (BRS) to ensure timely and accurate
financial reporting of the account Cash in Bank-Local Currency, Current
Account-111. She should make a representation, with the approval of the
Municipal Mayor, to the agency depository bank for the timely release of the bank
statements in order that she can prepare,update and submit the BRS on time. The
Municipal Mayor, being the head of the agency, should see to it that this
requirement should be complied by the Municipal Accountant being the
responsible officer to prepare the BRS. Moreover, she should initiate efforts to
address the problem of the latter by reducing her workloads and delegate them
to other personnel capable of doing the job in the finance department, in
accordance with Section 74 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and Sections 3.2 and
3.4 of the COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996.

We recommend the Local Chief Executive (LCE) should do the following:

a. The granting of loans shall be suspended until the Receivable from
OFWs will be collected;
b. Standard/criteria shall be set to serve as basis for the grants of OFW
loan assistance program beneficiaries if the loans will be granted again;
c. Strict compliance with the set standards for the pre-qualification of
recipients/beneficiaries must be observed;
d. The municipality must create an Oversight/Monitoring/Performance
Evaluation Committees to assure that the objectives of the program will be
attained;
e. The municipality should adopt measures to assure the repayments of these
loans such as sending notices or demand letters to delinquent ones and file
appropriate case, if any, for the non payment by the delinquent OFWs.
f. The Municipal Government should prepare monthly reports pertaining to
loans granted, collections made, as well as delinquencies.
g. The Municipal Government must also prepare an updated report on
delinquent beneficiaries and intensify collections for the loans granted to
beneficiaries of the program. The municipality should adopt measures to
assure the repayments of these loans such as sending notices or demand
letters to delinquent ones; and
h. Provide appropriate tools for easy and effective reports preparation.

We recommend to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to instruct the Municipal
Treasurer the following:
1. Municipal Treasurer
a. Abide to Section 57 of the Republic Act (R.A.) No. 464, otherwise
known as the Real Property Tax Code and Section 4 of the Republic
Act (R.A.) No. 5447, otherwise known as an Act creating a Special
Education Fund,
b. Conduct tax drives/ campaigns to every communities in the
Municipality,
c. Extra effort to collect the Real Property Tax (RPT) and
d. Recommend to its Sangguniang Bayan to legislate an ordinance that
would provide tax incentives to the real property owners who would
pay early, pursuant to Section 470 (d)(1) of the Republic Act (R.A.)
No. 7160, otherwise known as the Local Government Code of 1991.
2. Municipal Accountant
a. Adhere to Section 4 of the Republic Act (R.A.) No. 5447, otherwise
known as an Act creating the Special Education Fund
b. If any receivable from the General Fund, transfer it to the Special
Education Fund.

We recommend that Management:
• Create an Inventory Team to conduct the yearly physical inventory of
PPE, in compliance with Section 156 of the Commission on Audit
(COA) Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992.
• Reconcile the inventory report with the accounting and property
records for proper adjustment of the deficiencies if any to reflect the
accurate balances of the PPE accounts. To facilitate reconciliation, the
accountant must maintain the PPLEC as provided in Sections 119, 120
and 124 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System
(MNGAS) for Local Government Units, Volume 1.
• Comply with pursuant to paragraph 8 of the Philippine Public Sector
Accounting Standard (PPSAS) 17- Property, Plant and Equipment
(PPE).

We recommend that all deductions such as due to BIR/ PAG-IBIG/GSIS and
PHILHEALTH withheld from the salaries of employees should be remitted on
time to avoid penalties, surcharges, and other charges. We also require the
agency to submit strong justification why they cannot remit on the time frame
required, pursuant to Commission on Audit (COA) Memorandum No. 97-036.

The LGU should submit the subsidiary ledgers, data and information pertaining
to the account pursuant to the above stated guidelines to validate the accuracy
of its existence, in accordance with Section 6 (g) of Government Accounting
Manual for National Government Agencies, Volume I.

1. Require the implementing offices to exert diligent efforts to implement the
identified development projects in line with Section 287 of Republic Act
(R.A.) No. 7160, otherwise known as the Local Government Code of 1991
and Section 2.3 of the DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1
dated April 13, 2011, to ensure that the intended beneficiaries are not
deprived of the benefits that the should have enjoyed.
2. Enjoin offices concerned to evaluate whether the projects and programs are
implementable, otherwise revert to other more important with significant
impact projects to the constituents or beneficiaries.
3. Require those involved in the formulation, development and implementation
of programs/projects to faithfully discharge their functions and
responsibilities as provided in the Local Government Code and the Joint
Circular as above mentioned for maximum benefit, pursuant to Section 476
(b) of the Republic Act (R.A.) No. 7160, otherwise known as the Local
Government Code of 1991.

1445, otherwise known as the Government Auditing Code of the Philippines.
